content:
# Mark Chapter 13 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 39r–39v (PDF pages 78–79) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_013_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_013_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples exclaimed, "Teacher, look at these incredible stones! What magnificent buildings!"

² Jesus said, "You see all these great buildings? Not one stone will be left standing on another. Every one of them will be torn down."

³ Later, as Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives facing the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him privately.

⁴ "Tell us," they said, "when will this happen? And what will be the sign that all of this is about to take place?"

⁵ Jesus began: "Be careful that no one deceives you.

⁶ Many will come claiming to speak for me, saying, 'I am the one!' They will fool many people.

⁷ When you hear about wars and threats of wars, don't panic. These things must happen, but that's not the end.

⁸ Nations will go to war against each other, and kingdoms will clash. There will be earthquakes in many places and famines. But all of this is just the beginning — the first contractions before the birth.

⁹ Watch out for yourselves. You'll be dragged before courts and beaten in synagogues. You'll stand trial before governors and kings because of me, and your testimony will reach them.

¹⁰ But first, the good news must be announced to every nation.

¹¹ When they arrest you and put you on trial, don't worry ahead of time about what to say. Just speak whatever is given to you in that moment — because it won't be you speaking. It will be the Holy Spirit.

¹² Brothers will betray brothers to death. Fathers will turn against their own children. Children will rebel against their parents and have them killed.

¹³ Everyone will hate you because of me. But whoever stands firm to the end will be saved.

¹⁴ When you see the disgusting sacrilege standing where it has no right to stand — reader, pay attention! — then those in Judea must run for the hills.

¹⁵ If you're on your roof, don't go back inside to get anything.

¹⁶ If you're out in the field, don't go back for your coat.

¹⁷ How terrible it will be for pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!

¹⁸ Pray that this doesn't happen in winter.

¹⁹ Those will be days of suffering worse than anything the world has seen from the very beginning of God's creation until now — and nothing like it will ever happen again.

²⁰ If the Lord hadn't cut those days short, no one would survive. But for the sake of his chosen ones, he will shorten them.

²¹ If anyone tells you then, 'Look, here's the Messiah!' or 'There he is!' — don't believe it.

²² Fake messiahs and fake prophets will show up performing impressive signs and wonders, trying to deceive even God's chosen people if they can.

²³ So stay alert! I've warned you about everything ahead of time.

²⁴ In those days, after that terrible suffering, the sun will go dark and the moon will stop shining.

²⁵ Stars will fall from the sky, and the cosmic powers will be shaken loose.

²⁶ Then everyone will see the Son of Man arriving on the clouds with tremendous power and glory.

²⁷ He will send out his angels to gather his chosen ones from the four corners of the earth — from one end of the world to the other.

²⁸ Learn a lesson from the fig tree: when its branches grow tender and start putting out leaves, you know summer is close.

²⁹ In the same way, when you see all these things happening, you'll know that he is near — right at the door.

³⁰ I tell you the truth: this generation will not die out before all these things take place.

³¹ Heaven and earth will disappear, but my words will never disappear.

³² But as for the exact day or hour — nobody knows. Not the angels in heaven. Not even the Son. Only the Father.

³³ Stay alert! Keep watch! You don't know when the time will come.

³⁴ It's like a man leaving on a trip. He puts his servants in charge, assigns each one a task, and tells the doorkeeper to stay awake.

³⁵ So stay awake — because you don't know when the owner of the house will return. It could be evening, midnight, dawn, or early morning.

³⁶ Don't let him arrive suddenly and find you sleeping.

³⁷ What I'm telling you, I'm telling everyone: Stay awake!"

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

1. **The little apocalypse (chapter 13):** Mark's longest uninterrupted discourse. Jesus answers a simple question about the temple's destruction and ends up describing the end of the world. The two events — the fall of Jerusalem (70 AD) and the final coming — are layered on top of each other, and it's deliberately unclear where one ends and the other begins.

2. **"The disgusting sacrilege" (v. 14):** I render βδέλυγμα τῆς ἐρημώσεως dynamically rather than using the traditional "abomination of desolation," which is opaque to modern readers. The phrase comes from Daniel and originally referred to the pagan altar Antiochus IV placed in the temple. In Mark's context, it likely refers to the Roman destruction of the temple in 70 AD — or to a future desecration.

3. **"Let the reader understand" (v. 14):** This is Mark breaking the fourth wall. He's speaking directly to whoever is reading or hearing this text read aloud. It's a signal: this is coded language. Figure it out.

4. **"Nor the Son" (v. 32):** One of the most theologically explosive verses in the Gospels. Jesus says he doesn't know when the end will come. Later scribes and theologians struggled with this — some manuscripts delete "nor the Son." But Sinaiticus and Vaticanus both include it. Mark preserves it because it's what Jesus said.

5. **The four watches (v. 35):** Evening, midnight, rooster-crow, morning — these map exactly onto the four stages of the Passion narrative that follows: the Last Supper (evening), Gethsemane (midnight), Peter's denial (rooster-crow), the trial before Pilate (morning). Mark is planting seeds.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
